setting.note: The world of the novel is Owamboland, the flat communal farmland of far northern Namibia, where homesteads sit among the mahangu fields and the year turns on the rains. The narrator watches her friend's marriage and widowhood in the village of Oshaantu, while the regional towns near Oshakati draw the men away to wage work. The map runs from the fictional village out across the real Owambo lands to Oshakati and Ongwediva.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Neshani Andreas, first published 2001. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Purple Violet of Oshaantu, Neshani Andreas (2001). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The violet of the book's own title, for a story of women in the Owambo farmlands. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
